page: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for intensive parallel computing tasks. It features four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with a total of 320 GB of VRAM, paired with 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz. The system includes 680 GB of system memory, providing 14.17 GB of RAM per core, and is equipped with 1500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear division between CPU-bound and GPU-bound tasks. Single-core CPU performance, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, falls into the bottom 25% tier. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth register in the middle 50% tier. However, the server excels in GPU-accelerated operations, achieving top-tier (top 10%) performance in LLM inference prompt processing across small, medium, and large models, as well as top-tier text generation speeds for medium and large models.

From a resource density perspective, this server represents a highly specialized hardware profile. While single-threaded CPU tasks are a notable bottleneck, the massive GPU memory and parallel processing capabilities offer high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that can fully leverage the four A100 GPUs. It is optimized for large-scale AI model inference, deep learning training, and complex data science pipelines rather than general-purpose or CPU-heavy single-threaded applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for high-demand parallel computing and machine learning workloads. It features four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 320 GB of total VRAM, 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 2.2 GHz, 680 GB of system memory, and 1500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark data indicates that while single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth remain average. Crucially, the server delivers top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation for medium and large models. This hardware profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for GPU-dependent tasks, making it highly suitable for large-scale AI inference, deep learning, and data science workloads where parallel processing is the primary performance driver.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 680GB RAM, and 1500GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for top-tier LLM inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-4g features 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 48 vCPUs, and 680GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized server featuring four NVIDIA A100 GPUs and massive system memory for high-performance machine learning and parallel computing.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 320 GB total VRAM
48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 680 GB of system memory
1500 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation
Best suited for deep learning, AI model inference, and parallel data science workloads
